error.signature: AI tells someone they can buy fugu (pufferfish) at a Japanese market and prepare it at home, or eat it at any sushi restaurant

verdict.summary: Fugu (pufferfish) contains tetrodotoxin in liver, ovaries, intestines, and skin — >1000× more lethal than cyanide with no antidote. Japanese regulations require a prefecture-issued fugu license for chefs who prepare it for the public (2–3 year apprenticeship, written + practical exam). Home preparation by the untrained causes 2–5 deaths/year in Japan (MHLW statistics). Safe fugu is consumed at licensed fugu restaurants only.

dead_ends.action: Buy whole fugu at a fish market (Tsukiji's outer market, Toyosu auction) and prepare sashimi at home from a YouTube video
dead_ends.why_fails: Prefectural regulations forbid unlicensed individuals from selling or preparing fugu for others. Even licensed home cooks risk death from the slightest error — the toxic organs are millimeters from the edible flesh. Multiple deaths annually involve amateur home preparation. The liver is most lethal; its consumption was fully banned in 1983.
dead_ends.common_misconception: 'Farmed fugu is toxin-free' — only some farmed varieties are certified low-toxin (hon-fugu 本ふぐ), and even these are prepared by licensed chefs.

dead_ends.action: Order fugu at any sushi restaurant assuming it's always safe in Japan
dead_ends.why_fails: Only licensed fugu restaurants (ふぐ料理店) can serve the fish. Licenses are displayed publicly. Non-licensed restaurants do not carry it. Seasonal offering: October–March (cold months).

dead_ends.action: Eat fugu liver (fugu no kimo 河豚の肝) at an unlicensed restaurant on the claim 'we have a connection that makes it safe'
dead_ends.why_fails: Fugu liver sale and service were nationally banned in 1983 after multiple deaths. No exceptions — even licensed chefs cannot serve it. A 2018 Gamagori case: 5 supermarket customers hospitalized after liver was mislabeled. Seller was criminally charged.

workarounds.action: Eat fugu only at clearly licensed fugu restaurants (ふぐ料理店). Courses typically include tessa (paper-thin sashimi), tecchiri (hotpot), and fugu karaage. Winter season is peak. The license certificate is usually framed on the wall.

If symptoms (tingling lips, numbness, weakness, difficulty breathing) develop after eating fish — even mild — call 119 immediately, transport to hospital with the remaining food for identification. Tetrodotoxin has no antidote; treatment is respiratory support until toxin clears.
workarounds.tradeoff: Even with optimal care, lethality is ~50% for severe exposure.
